sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Tricyclic antidepressants (TCAs) are used to treat depression, chronic pain, and other psychiatric or neurological conditions and be common medication. Examples include Examples include amitriptyline, nortriptyline, imipramine, and clomipramine. TCA toxicity most commonly results from intentional overdose and can be a life-threatening medical emergency due to its cardiotoxic and neurotoxic effects.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Arrhythmias (tachycardia, heart blocks, VF, VT, asystole), ECG changes, Hypotension
- Reduced level of consciousness, agitation, confusion, nystagmus
- Dry, warm, flushed skin, hyperthermia, dilated pupils
- Hyperreflexia, seizure, coma, death

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Investigating why or how the overdose occurred is important to rule out self-harm
- About 10 mg/kg or more can potentially be life-threatening
- Large overdoses can lead rapidly to seizures, coma, hypotension, and death
- QRS widening from sodium channel blockade is a major red flag; more than 100 ms predicts higher seizure risk and more than 160 ms predicts ventricular arrhythmia risk
- Manage cardiac arrest or malignant ventricular dysrhythmias with standard resuscitation measures, including defibrillation when indicated, while simultaneously treating sodium channel blockade with sodium bicarbonate
- Amiodarone, beta-blockers, and routine antiarrhythmics that worsen sodium channel blockade are generally avoided

sections.title: Management – Primary
sections.content:
- Primary Survey
- Offer continuous support and reassurance if applicable
- Position patient appropriately
- Administer oxygen if indicated
- Record vital signs and obtain early rhythm assessment if feasible
- Consider activated charcoal only if presentation is early, the airway is protected, and this is consistent with local protocol
- Have a low threshold for calling the on-call doctor

sections.title: Management – Advanced
sections.content:
- Consider advanced airway early if conscious state is falling or a significant TCA ingestion with evolving toxicity is suspected
- Provide assisted ventilation if required
- Apply continuous cardiac monitoring and obtain 12 lead ECG to assess for sodium channel blockade
- Gain IV access
- Consider blood testing where available and clinically useful
- Consider IV fluids if indicated for hypotension
- Consider sodium bicarbonate 8.4% in consultation with the on-call doctor for QRS widening, ventricular dysrhythmia, hypotension, or cardiac arrest consistent with TCA toxicity
- Use IM or IV midazolam for seizures in line with scope or protocol

sections.title: Additional Information
sections.content: - Always offer engagement with Mind Matters or EAP if applicable
